text:
Epilepsy Centre 

Neurosciences Division 

Clinic Date: 22 July 2014 

Dr Patel  
St George's University Hospitals NHS Foundation Trust  
Blackshaw Road, London, SW17 0QT  

Dear Dr Patel,  

Emily Rogers, DOB: 14-06-2001, Hospital No: H927451 NHS No. 7482103695  
58 Willow Close, Sutton, SM2 7LP  

Diagnoses: 

Seizure disorder, aetiology not established. 

Thank you for reviewing Emily Rogers, who attended today with her mother. Since her last appointment three months ago, she describes a sustained improvement in day-to-day seizure control following VNS optimisation at the previous visit. Seizure activity is infrequent. She reports one absence seizure every 3 week and has been free of tonic-clonic seizures for over nine months. Occasional morning myoclonic jerks continue, though they are mild and do not interfere significantly with daily function. She and her mother feel the pattern has been stable and clearly better compared to earlier in the year. 

She reports good adherence to her current antiseizure medication regimen without adverse effects beyond mild, intermittent fatigue. Sleep hygiene has improved with a consistent bedtime, and she notes reduced stress since returning to part-time college studies. She carries her magnet and uses it rarely; when employed promptly for prodromal symptoms, she perceives faster recovery and shorter episodes. No injuries, emergency attendances, or use of rescue medication have occurred since the last review. 

VNS was interrogated in clinic today. Diagnostics were satisfactory with stable lead impedance at 2325 ohms, battery status not at elective replacement indicator, and normal mode functioning as expected. No parameter changes were made, as the current clinical response is favourable. Magnet usage logs show infrequent activation in keeping with her report. 

On examination today, she was alert and oriented with normal speech and gait. Neurological examination was unremarkable. 

Plan: 
- Continue current antiseizure medication unchanged given clinical improvement and tolerability. 
- Maintain current VNS settings in the context of perceived benefit post-optimisation. 
- Safety advice reinforced, including consistent magnet carriage and seizure first-aid. 
- We will review in six months, or sooner if there is any deterioration, breakthrough tonic-clonic activity, or concerns regarding VNS function. 

Please do not hesitate to contact me if further information is required. 

Yours sincerely

entities.text: Seizure activity is infrequent. She reports one absence seizure every 3 week and has been free of tonic-clonic seizures for over nine months. Occasional morning myoclonic jerks continue, though they are mild and do not interfere significantly with daily function

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter explicitly states current seizure occurrences: "one absence seizure every 3 week" and "Occasional morning myoclonic jerks continue" but without a numeric rate. It also notes she has been free of tonic-clonic seizures for over nine months. Per rule 7, select the highest frequency among provided figures; the only explicit numeric frequency is 1 absence seizure every 3 weeks. The myoclonic jerks lack a quantifiable frequency, so they cannot be used. Therefore, the current seizure frequency is 1 per 3 weeks.
reference: Seizure activity is infrequent. She reports one absence seizure every 3 week and has been free of tonic-clonic seizures for over nine months. Occasional morning myoclonic jerks continue, though they are mild and do not interfere significantly with daily function
